classes.label: Conference
classes.description: A conference event with temporal and spatial properties

data_properties.label: has conference name
data_properties.description: The name or title of the conference

data_properties.label: has conference URI
data_properties.description: A URI reference to the conference resource

data_properties.label: has conference date
data_properties.description: The date of the conference as a string representation

data_properties.label: has conference location
data_properties.description: The location or venue of the conference

data_properties.label: has conference datum
data_properties.description: The timestamp of the conference event

class_mappings.description: Maps each row in conferences table to a Conference instance. No row-selection condition identified; all rows represent valid conference entities.

open_issues.description: Date column is varchar(50) rather than timestamp; unclear if it contains structured date strings, free-text descriptions, or ISO format dates
Location column flagged as address-like but no separate location entity table exists; unclear if location should be modeled as a separate class or remain as literal
Relationship between Date (varchar) and Datum (timestamp) columns unclear; both appear to represent temporal information but with different types and purposes
What are sample values in the Date column? Are they ISO date strings, free-text descriptions, or structured date formats?

open_issues.description: {'issue_type': 'datatype_format_ambiguity', 'related_table': 'conferences', 'related_column': 'date', 'description': 'Date column is varchar(50) rather than timestamp; unclear if it contains ISO date strings, free-text descriptions, structured date formats, or date ranges. Affects interpretation of hasConferenceDate property.'}

{'issue_type': 'semantic_distinction_ambiguity', 'related_table': 'conferences', 'related_columns': ['date', 'datum'], 'description': 'Relationship between Date (varchar) and Datum (timestamp) columns unclear. Both represent temporal information but with different types. Possible interpretations: Date is display/description, Datum is system timestamp; or Date is event date, Datum is record creation time; or both represent same event with different precision.'}

{'issue_type': 'location_modeling_ambiguity', 'related_table': 'conferences', 'related_column': 'location', 'description': 'Location column flagged as address-like but no separate location entity table exists in current scope. Unclear if location should be modeled as separate Location class (pending discovery of related tables) or remain as literal string property.'}
